title: 🧭 Your AI Learning Journey,
subtitle: Learn How AI Works by Building It

steps.title: 👋 Welcome to the AI Literacy Journey
steps.body:
Artificial Intelligence is changing the world, but before we can build AI systems, we first need to understand how they work.

In this curriculum, you will learn AI step by step, from the very beginning to modern AI systems.

steps.title: 💡What You Will Learn
steps.body:
# 🧠 Human Intelligence vs Artificial Intelligence



Discover what intelligence is and how humans learn, think, solve problems, and make decisions.

Explore how Artificial Intelligence was inspired by human intelligence and compare how humans and machines solve problems differently.





# 📖 The First AI Systems: Rule-Based AI



Learn how early AI systems worked using rules such as **IF**, **AND**, **OR**, and **NOT**.

🧪 **Build Rule-Based Systems in ABITA**

Create smart systems such as alarms, doors, fans, lights, and automated rooms using rules.





# 📖 Why Rule-Based AI Has Limitations



Discover the challenges of rule-based systems and why they struggle with complex real-world problems.

🧪 **Test the Limits of Rule-Based AI**

Build rule-based systems in **ABITA** and explore situations where rules start to fail.





# 📖 Modern AI: Machine Learning



Learn how modern AI systems learn from examples instead of following fixed rules.

Understand why Machine Learning became the foundation of today's AI systems.

🧪 **Train AI Models in ABITA**

Teach AI systems by providing examples instead of writing rules.





# 📖 Data and AI Models



Learn how data helps AI discover patterns and make predictions.

Explore different types of data and understand how AI models learn from them.

🧪 **Build and Improve AI Models**

Create datasets, train image models, train text models, test their performance, and improve their accuracy.

steps.title: 🎯 Learn by Doing
steps.body:
Throughout this curriculum, you will find:

- **Mini Lessons** to build your understanding of AI concepts.
- **Guided Labs** that show you exactly how to use AI tools in ABITA.
- **Challenge Labs** where you apply your knowledge independently.



By the end of this journey, you will not only understand how AI works, but you will also be able to **collect data, train models, test AI systems, and improve their performance using ABITA**.

steps.title: 🤖 Your Journey into AI
steps.body:
Every AI system was inspired by intelligence.
Let's start by exploring what intelligence really is.


🚀 See you in Chapter 1: Understanding Intelligence.

steps.title: 1. 🧠 What is Intelligence?
steps.body:
**Intelligence** is the ability to:

📚 **Learn**

🤔 **Think**

✅ **Make Decisions**




There are **two types of intelligence** we will learn about in this course:

🧠 **Human Intelligence**

🤖 **Artificial Intelligence (AI)**




Let's explore both!

steps.title: 2. 🧠 Human Intelligence
steps.body:
Human Intelligence helps us:

📚 **Learn from Experience**

🤔 **Think About Situations**

✅ **Make Decisions**


Let's see how humans use intelligence every day.






## 📚 Learn from Experience

Imagine you are riding your bicycle.

🚲 One day, you hit a small obstacle and almost fall.

The next day, when you see the same obstacle, you go around it.

👉 **You learned from your experience.**







## 🤔 Think About Situations

Imagine it is raining outside.

🌧️ Before leaving home, you think:

- **Should I take an umbrella?**
- **What if it rains more?**
- **Will I get wet?**

After thinking about the situation, you prepare accordingly.

👉 **You used intelligence to think before acting.**







## ✅ Make Decisions 

Imagine you have:

📖 **Homework to complete**

🎮 **A game you want to play**

You decide to finish your homework first and play later.

👉 **You made a decision.**

steps.title: 3. 🌟 Human Intelligence Summary
steps.body:
Every day, humans use intelligence to:

📚 **Learn from Experience**

🤔 **Think About Situations**

✅ **Make Decisions**

Whether we are studying, playing sports, or solving problems, **we use intelligence all the time.**

steps.title: 4. 🤖 Artificial Intelligence (AI)
steps.body:
**Artificial Intelligence (AI)** is intelligence created by humans.

AI can:

📋 **Follow Instructions**

📚 **Learn from Data**

✅ **Make Decisions or Predictions**


Let's see how AI works.



---



## 1. 📋 Follow Instructions

Imagine you tell a robot:

**"If the room is dark, turn on the light."**

The robot follows your instruction.

👉 **It is doing exactly what you told it to do.**







## 2.  📚 Learn from Data

Imagine an AI sees thousands of pictures of cats.

🐱 🐱 🐱 🐱

Over time, it learns what cats usually look like.

👉 **It learned from data.**







## 3. ✅ Make Decisions or Predictions

Imagine you watch cricket videos every day on YouTube.

🏏 🏏 🏏

Soon, YouTube starts recommending more cricket videos.



👉 **The AI predicts what you might like to watch.**

steps.title: 5. 🌟 Artificial Intelligence Summary
steps.body:
AI can:

📋 **Follow Instructions**

📚 **Learn from Data**

✅ **Make Decisions or Predictions**


AI can perform some tasks that appear intelligent, but it is created by humans.

steps.title: 6. 🚀 Why Do We Build AI?
steps.body:
The goal of AI is to **mimic some parts of human intelligence**.

Why?

⚡ **To Save Time**

📈 **To Improve Efficiency**

🔍 **To Process Large Amounts of Information**

🤝 **To Help Humans Solve Problems**





## 🌟 Remember

🧠 **Humans create AI.**

🤖 **AI helps humans.**

👉 **AI is a tool designed to help us solve problems and make life easier.**

steps.title: 7. 🤔 How Does AI Know What To Do?
steps.body:
We learned that AI can:


- 📋 **Follow Instructions**

- 📚 **Learn from Data**

- ✅ **Make Decisions or Predictions**




| 🧠 Human Intelligence | 🤖 Artificial Intelligence (AI) |
| --- | --- |
| Learns from Experience | Learns from Data |
| Thinks About Situations | Follows Instructions |
| Makes Decisions | Makes Decisions or Predictions |




But who teaches AI what to do?

👉 Humans do!

AI follows instructions called **rules**.



🌟 In the next chapter, we will learn how to teach computers using rules.

steps.title: A Quick History of AI
steps.body:
In 1956, scientists started exploring how computers could become intelligent.

One of their first ideas was simple: teach computers to follow rules.



For example:

- If a **Switch** is ON → Turn the **Bulb** ON.
- If **Motion** is detected → Turn the **Fan** ON.
- If the **Temperature** is above **35°C** → Turn the **AC** ON.


This approach became known as **Rule-Based AI**.



In this chapter, you will build your own Rule-Based AI systems using ABITA.

steps.title: 1. 💡Turn On the Bulb
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to connect an input to an action.



## Problem
If the **switch** is **ON**, the **bulb** should turn **ON**.


## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 3:**
Connect **Switch** to **Bulb**.

**Step 4:**
Turn **ON** the **switch** and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the **switch** is **ON**, the **bulb** turns **ON**.
✅ When the **switch** is **OFF**, the **bulb** turns **OFF**.




## What You Learned

1. Inputs can control actions.
2. Components can be connected together to create a simple smart system.

steps.title: 2. 🌀Turn On the Fan
steps.instruction:
## Problem

If the **Switch** is **ON**, the **Fan** should turn **ON**.




## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 3:**
Connect **Switch** to **Fan**.

**Step 4:**
Turn **ON** the **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 5:**
Turn **OFF** the **Switch** and observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When the **Switch** is **ON**, the **Fan** turns **ON**.

✅ When the **Switch** is **OFF**, the **Fan** turns **OFF**.





## What You Learned

1. A **Switch** can control different devices.

2. Inputs can be connected directly to actions to build simple smart systems.

steps.title: 3. 🚪Open the Door
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to control different devices using the same input.


## Problem

If the **switch** is **ON**, the **door** should **open**.




## Instructions

**Step 1: ** Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
**Step 2:** Drag **Door** from the **Actions** menu.
**Step 3:** Connect **Switch** to **Door**.
**Step 4:** Turn **ON** the **switch** and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the **switch** is **ON**, the **door** opens.
✅ When the **switch** is **OFF**, the **door** closes.




## What You Learned

1. The same **input** can control different **actions**.
2. Computers can automate devices such as **lights**, **fans**, and **doors**.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 1: Smart Classroom Starter
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

A teacher wants the classroom to be ready when students arrive.

Turn ON both the **Bulb** and **Fan** using a single **Switch**.

---

**Requirements**

- Use one **Switch**.
- Use a **Bulb**.
- Use a **Fan**.

---

**Success Criteria**

✅ Both the **Bulb** and **Fan** turn ON when the **Switch** is ON.

✅ Both devices turn OFF when the **Switch** is OFF.

steps.title: 4. 🚶Motion Light
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use an **IF Condition** with a **Motion Sensor**.




## Problem

If **motion** is detected, turn the **bulb** ON.




## Instructions

- Drag **Motion Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions** menu.
- Connect **Motion Sensor** to the **IF Condition**.
- Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Bulb**.
- Double-click the **IF Condition**.
- Set the condition to **Motion Detected**.
- Double-click the **Motion Sensor**.
- Click the **No Motion** button to change its state.
- Observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When **Motion** is detected, the **bulb** turns **ON**.
✅ When **No Motion** is detected, the **bulb** remains **OFF**.




**What You Learned**

1. An **IF Condition** helps computers make decisions.
2. Sensors provide information that can be used in rules.
steps.canvasHint: Click on a block to select it, its settings appear in the Properties panel on the right.

steps.title: 5. 🌬️Smart Fan
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how the same **IF Condition** can control different devices.




## Problem

If **motion** is detected, turn the **fan** ON.




## Instructions

- Drag **Motion Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.
- Connect **Motion Sensor** to the **IF Condition**.
- Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Fan**.
- Double-click the **IF Condition**.
- Set the condition to **Motion Detected**.
- Double-click the **Motion Sensor**.
- Click the **No Motion** button to change its state.
- Observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When **Motion** is detected, the **fan** turns **ON**.
✅ When **No Motion** is detected, the **fan** remains **OFF**.




## What You Learned

1. The same **IF Condition** can control different devices.
2. Rules can be reused to automate many actions.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 2: Smart Study Lamp
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Turn the **Bulb** ON when **Motion** is detected.



**Requirements**

- Use a **Motion Sensor**.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use a **Bulb**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **Bulb** turns **ON** when **Motion** is detected.

✅ The **Bulb** remains **OFF** when there is **No Motion**.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 3: Welcome Home System
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Turn ON both the **Bulb** and **Fan** when **Motion** is detected.



**Requirements**

- Use a **Motion Sensor**.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use a **Bulb**.
- Use a **Fan**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ Both the **Bulb** and **Fan** turn **ON** when **Motion** is detected.

✅ Both devices remain **OFF** when there is **No Motion**.

steps.title: 6. 🥵Hot Room Alert
steps.instruction:
## Problem

If the **temperature** is greater than **35°C**, turn the **fan** ON.




## Instructions

- Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.
- Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.
- Connect **Temperature Sensor** to the **IF Condition**.
- Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Fan**.
- Double-click the **IF Condition**.
- Set the condition to **Temperature > 35°C**.
- Double-click the **Temperature Sensor**.
- Move the slider to **30°C** and observe the result.
- Move the slider to **36°C** and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the **temperature** is **30°C**, the **fan** remains **OFF**.
✅ When the **temperature** is **36°C**, the **fan** turns **ON**.




## What You Learned

- An **IF Condition** can compare values.
- Computers can make decisions based on sensor readings.

steps.title: 7. ❄️Hot Room Cooling System
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how one **IF Condition** can control multiple devices.



## Problem

If the **temperature** is greater than **40°C**, turn **ON** the **Fan** and the **AC**.



## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**
Drag **AC** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 5:**
Connect **Temperature Sensor** to the **IF Condition**.

**Step 6:**
Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Fan**.

**Step 7:**
Connect the **IF Condition** to the **AC**.

**Step 8:**
Double-click the **IF Condition**.

**Step 9:**
Set the condition to **Temperature > 40°C**.

**Step 10:**
Double-click the **Temperature Sensor**.

**Step 11:**
Move the slider to **35°C** and observe the result.

**Step 12:**
Move the slider to **41°C** and observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When the **temperature** is **35°C**, the **Fan** and **AC** remain **OFF**.

✅ When the **temperature** is **41°C**, the **Fan** and **AC** turn **ON**.





## What You Learned

1. One **IF Condition** can control multiple devices.
2. A single rule can trigger multiple actions.

steps.title: 8. 🔑Password Protected Door
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use **Text Input** with an **IF Condition**.




## Problem

If the password is **Pakistan**, open the **Door**.




## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Text Input** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **Door** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**
Connect **Text Input** to the **IF Condition**.

**Step 5:**
Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Door**.

**Step 6:**
Double-click the **IF Condition**.

**Step 7:**
Set the condition to **Text = Pakistan**.

**Step 8:**
Double-click the **Text Input**.

**Step 9:**
Enter **pakistan** and observe the result.

**Step 10:**
Enter **Pakistan** and observe the result.






## Expected Result

✅ When the text is **pakistan**, the **Door** remains **closed**.

✅ When the text is **Pakistan**, the **Door** opens.





## What You Learned

1. Text can be used as an input for rules.

2. An **IF Condition** can compare words as well as numbers.

3. Text matching is **case-sensitive**, so **Pakistan** and **pakistan** are treated as different values.

steps.title: 9. 👋Smart Greeting Detector
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use the **Contains** condition with **Text Input**.




## Problem

If the text contains the word **hello**, turn the **Bulb** ON.




## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Text Input** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**
Connect **Text Input** to the **IF Condition**.

**Step 5:**
Connect the **IF Condition** to the **Bulb**.

**Step 6:**
Double-click the **IF Condition** to edit it.

**Step 7:**
From the **Condition** dropdown, select **Contains**.

**Step 8:**
In the **Value to compare** field, type **hello**.

**Step 9:**
Double-click the **Text Input** component.

**Step 10:**
Enter **Good morning everyone** and observe the result.

**Step 11:**
Enter **hello everyone** and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the text is **Good morning everyone**, the **Bulb** remains **OFF**.

✅ When the text is **hello everyone**, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.




## What You Learned

1. The **Contains** condition can search for a word inside a sentence.

2. A sentence can trigger an action even when it contains additional words.

3. Text matching is **case-sensitive**, so **hello** and **Hello** are treated as different values.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 4: Secure Door
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Open the **Door** only when the password is **Pakistan**.



**Requirements**

- Use **Text Input**.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use a **Door**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **Door** opens when the text is **Pakistan**.

✅ The **Door** remains closed for any other text.

steps.title: 10. 🎚️Two Switch AC System
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use an **AND Condition**.





## Problem

Turn the **AC** ON only when **both switches** are ON.




## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag another **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **AND Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**
Drag **AC** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 5:**
Connect the first **Switch** to the **AND Condition**.

**Step 6:**
Connect the second **Switch** to the **AND Condition**.

**Step 7:**
Connect the **AND Condition** to the **AC**.

**Step 8:**
Turn ON only the first **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 9:**
Turn ON only the second **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 10:**
Turn ON both **Switches** and observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When only one **Switch** is ON, the **AC** remains **OFF**.

✅ When both **Switches** are ON, the **AC** turns **ON**.





## What You Learned

1. An **AND Condition** requires all conditions to be true.

2. The output turns ON only when both inputs are ON.

steps.title: 11. 💡Backup Light System
steps.instruction:
## Goal
  
Learn how to use an **OR Condition**.



## Problem
  
A room has **two switches**.

The **Light** should turn ON if **either Switch A OR Switch B** is ON.

This way, if one switch fails, the other switch can still turn the Light ON.





## Instructions

**Step 1:**  
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**  
Drag another **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**  
Drag **OR Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**  
Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions menu**.

**Step 5:**  
Connect the first **Switch** to the **OR Condition**.

**Step 6:**  
Connect the second **Switch** to the **OR Condition**.

**Step 7:**  
Connect the **OR Condition** to the **Bulb**.

**Step 8:**  
Turn ON only the first **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 9:**  
Turn ON only the second **Switch** and observe the result.

**Step 10:**  
Turn OFF both **Switches** and observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When the first **Switch** is ON, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.

✅ When the second **Switch** is ON, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.

✅ When both **Switches** are ON, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.

❌ When both **Switches** are OFF, the **Bulb** remains **OFF**.





## What You Learned

1. An **OR Condition** requires at least one condition to be true.

2. The output turns **ON** when either input is **ON**.

3. **OR Conditions** are useful for creating backup systems.

steps.title: 12. 🛡️Security Door
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to use a **NOT Condition**.





## Problem

Open the **Door** only when the **Switch** is OFF.



## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Switch** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **NOT Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **Door** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**
Connect the **Switch** to the **NOT Condition**.

**Step 5:**
Connect the **NOT Condition** to the **Door**.

**Step 6:**
Turn the **Switch** OFF and observe the result.

**Step 7:**
Turn the **Switch** ON and observe the result.




## Expected Result

✅ When the **Switch** is **OFF**, the **Door** opens.

✅ When the **Switch** is **ON**, the **Door** remains closed.





## What You Learned

1. A **NOT Condition** reverses a result.

2. If something is **TRUE**, **NOT** makes it **FALSE**.

3. If something is **FALSE**, **NOT** makes it **TRUE**.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 5: Security Approval System
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Turn the **AC** ON only when:

- **Switch A** is ON.
- **Switch B** is ON.
- **Motion** is detected.



**Requirements**

- Use **2 Switches**.
- Use a **Motion Sensor**.
- Use **IF Conditions**.
- Use **2** **AND Conditions**.
- Use an **AC**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **AC** turns **ON** only when all three conditions are TRUE.

✅ The **AC** remains **OFF** if any condition is FALSE.

steps.title: 13. 🌡️Comfort Zone Temperature Control
steps.instruction:
## Goal
Learn how to combine two **IF Conditions** using an **AND Condition**.




## Problem

Turn the **Fan** ON only when the **temperature** is between **30°C** and **35°C**.





## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag another **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**
Drag **AND Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 5:**
Drag **Fan** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 6:**
Connect the **Temperature Sensor** to both **IF Conditions**.

**Step 7:**
Connect both **IF Conditions** to the **AND Condition**.

**Step 8:**
Connect the **AND Condition** to the **Fan**.

**Step 9:**
Double-click the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 10:**
Set the condition to **Temperature > 30°C**.

**Step 11:**
Double-click the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 12:**
Set the condition to **Temperature < 35°C**.

**Step 13:**
Double-click the **Temperature Sensor**.

**Step 14:**
Move the slider to **25°C** and observe the result.

**Step 15:**
Move the slider to **32°C** and observe the result.

**Step 16:**
Move the slider to **40°C** and observe the result.






## Expected Result

✅ When the **temperature** is **25°C**, the **Fan** remains **OFF**.

✅ When the **temperature** is **32°C**, the **Fan** turns **ON**.

✅ When the **temperature** is **40°C**, the **Fan** remains **OFF**.






## What You Learned

- Two **IF Conditions** can be combined using an **AND Condition**.

- The **AND Condition** becomes TRUE only when both conditions are TRUE.

- Computers can use ranges to make smarter decisions.

steps.title: 14. 🏠Occupied Room Cooling System
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to combine multiple **IF Conditions** using an **AND Condition**.



## Problem

Turn the **AC** ON only when **motion is detected** and the **temperature** is greater than **35°C**.



## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Motion Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**
Drag another **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 5:**
Drag **AND Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 6:**
Drag **AC** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 7:**
Connect the **Motion Sensor** to the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 8:**
Connect the **Temperature Sensor** to the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 9:**
Connect both **IF Conditions** to the **AND Condition**.

**Step 10:**
Connect the **AND Condition** to the **AC**.

**Step 11:**
Double-click the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 12:**
Set the condition to **Motion Detected**.

**Step 13:**
Double-click the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 14:**
Set the condition to **Temperature > 35°C**.

**Step 15:**
Double-click the **Motion Sensor** and set it to **No Motion**.

**Step 16:**
Double-click the **Temperature Sensor** and move the slider to **40°C**.

**Step 17:**
Observe the result.

**Step 18:**
Change the **Motion Sensor** to **Motion Detected**.

**Step 19:**
Observe the result.






## Expected Result


✅ When the **temperature** is **40°C** but there is **No Motion**, the **AC** remains **OFF**.

✅ When **Motion** is detected and the **temperature** is **40°C**, the **AC** turns **ON**.




## What You Learned

Multiple **IF Conditions** can be combined using an **AND Condition**.

The **AND Condition** becomes TRUE only when all connected conditions are TRUE.

Smart systems often use multiple sensors before taking action.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 6: Comfort Zone Fan
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Turn the **Fan** ON only when the temperature is between **30°C** and **35°C**.



**Requirements**

- Use a **Temperature Sensor**.
- Use **2 IF Conditions**.
- Use an **AND Condition**.
- Use a **Fan**.



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **Fan** turns **ON** when the temperature is between **30°C** and **35°C**.

✅ The **Fan** remains **OFF** outside this range.

steps.title: 15. 🏡Smart Room Assistant
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to build a smart system using multiple rules.





## Problem

- If **motion** is detected, turn the **Bulb** ON.
- If the **temperature** is greater than **35°C**, turn the **AC** ON.





## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Drag **Motion Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 2:**
Drag **Temperature Sensor** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 3:**
Drag **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 4:**
Drag another **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based menu**.

**Step 5:**
Drag **Bulb** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 6:**
Drag **AC** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 7:**
Connect the **Motion Sensor** to the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 8:**
Connect the first **IF Condition** to the **Bulb**.

**Step 9:**
Connect the **Temperature Sensor** to the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 10:**
Connect the second **IF Condition** to the **AC**.

**Step 11:**
Double-click the first **IF Condition**.

**Step 12:**
Set the condition to **Motion Detected**.

**Step 13:**
Double-click the second **IF Condition**.

**Step 14:**
Set the condition to **Temperature > 35°C**.

**Step 15:**
Double-click the **Motion Sensor** and set it to **Motion Detected**.

**Step 16:**
Double-click the **Temperature Sensor** and move the slider to **40°C**.

**Step 17:**
Observe the result.





## Expected Result

✅ When **Motion** is detected, the **Bulb** turns **ON**.

✅ When the **temperature** is above **35°C**, the **AC** turns **ON**.

✅ Both rules work independently at the same time.





## What You Learned

1. A smart system can contain multiple rules.

2. Different sensors can control different devices.

3. Complex systems are built by combining simple rules.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 7: Smart Classroom
steps.prompt:
**Problem**

Create a **Smart Classroom** that automatically controls lights and cooling.



**Requirements**

- If **Motion** is detected → Turn **Bulb** ON.
- If **Temperature > 35°C** → Turn **Fan** ON.
- If **Temperature > 40°C** → Turn **AC** ON.



**Allowed Components**

- **1 Motion Sensor**
- **1 Temperature Sensor**
- **1 Bulb**
- **1 Fan**
- **1 AC**
- Any number of **IF Conditions**
- Any number of **AND**, **OR**, and **NOT Conditions**



**Success Criteria**

✅ The **Bulb** responds to **Motion**.

✅ The **Fan** responds to **Temperature**.

✅ The **AC** responds to higher **Temperature**.

✅ Only the allowed components are used.

✅ All rules work together correctly.

steps.title: 16. 📌Summary
steps.body:
Great job!

In this chapter, you learned that:



📋 **AI follows instructions.**

🔀 **Rules use IF–THEN logic.**

🤖 **AI follows rules exactly as they are written.**

✅ **The same input produces the same output.**

✅ **Rules work well in simple and predictable situations.**




Using ABITA, you built your own **rule-based AI systems** and taught AI how to respond to different situations.



🌟 **You are now ready to explore a bigger question: Can rules solve every problem?**

steps.title: 1. 🤔 When Rules Are Not Enough
steps.body:
In the last chapter, you taught AI using **rules**.

You learned that rules can help AI make decisions.

But can rules solve **every problem**?


Let's find out!

steps.title: 2. ⚠️ What is a Spam Detector?
steps.body:
# 📧 What is a Spam Detector?

Imagine your inbox receives 100 messages today.

Some messages are important:

📧 "Your teacher shared today's homework."

📧 "Your football match starts at 5 PM."





But some messages are unwanted:

📧 "Congratulations! You won a prize!"

📧 "Click here to claim your reward!"

📧 "You have won $1,000,000!"




A Spam Detector helps separate unwanted messages from important ones so we do not miss something important.

steps.title: 3. 🚀 Your Mission
steps.body:
🚀 **Your Mission**

Can you build a spam detector using only **rules**?



👉 In this chapter, we will discover whether simple rules are enough to build a good spam detector.

steps.title: 4. Guided Lab 1: Detect a Spam Word
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how a rule can be used to detect a spam message.




## Problem

You work for an email company.

Your security team has discovered that many spam emails contain the word:



**MONEY**

Create a spam detector that triggers the Alarm whenever this word appears.




## Instructions


**Step 1:**

Drag a **Text Input** from the **Rule-Based** menu.

**Step 2:**

Drag an **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based** menu.

**Step 3:**

Drag an **Alarm** from the **Actions** menu.

**Step 4:**

Connect:

**Text Input → IF Condition**

**IF Condition → Alarm**

**Step 5:**

Double-click the **IF Condition**.

Configure it as:

**Contains: MONEY**

**Step 6:**

Go to the **Text Input** and type:

**MONEY**

Observe the result.

**Step 7:**

Now type:

**mOneY**

Observe the result.

The Alarm should remain silent.



## Success Criteria

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONEY**.

✅ Alarm remains OFF for messages that do not contain **MONEY**.

steps.title: 5. Guided Lab 2: Spammers Found a New Variation
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how rule-based systems need additional rules when new variations appear.




## Problem

Your spam detector successfully detects: **MONEY**

However, spammers have discovered a way to bypass your system.

Instead of writing: **MONEY**

they now write: **MonEy**

Update your spam detector so it can detect both versions.




## Instructions

**Step 1:**

Open your existing spam detector.

**Step 2:**

Drag a new **IF Condition** from the **Rule-Based** menu.

**Step 3:**

Drag an **OR Condition** from the **Rule-Based** menu.

**Step 4:**

Connect both **IF Conditions** to the **OR Condition**.

**Step 5:**

Connect the **OR Condition** to the **Alarm**.

**Step 6:**

Double-click the new **IF Condition**.

Configure it as:

**Contains: MonEy**

**Step 7:**

Go to the **Text Input** and type:

**MONEY**

Observe the result.

The Alarm should turn ON.

**Step 8:**

Now type:

**MonEy**

Observe the result.

The Alarm should turn ON.



## Success Criteria

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONEY**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MonEy**.

✅ Alarm remains OFF for other messages.




## Expected Result

🤔 Your detector can now catch two variations of the same spam word, but it requires additional rules to do so.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 1: Improve the Spam Detector
steps.prompt:
## Problem

Spammers have created another variation:

**money**

Update your spam detector so it can detect:

- **MONEY**
- **MonEy**
- **money**



## Requirements

- Use the existing spam detector.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use **OR Conditions** if required.
- Use an **Alarm**.



## Success Criteria

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONEY**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MonEy**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **money**.

✅ Alarm remains OFF for messages that do not contain these words.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 2: Keep Up with the Spammers
steps.prompt:
## Problem

Spammers have created another variation:

**MONey**

Update your spam detector so it can detect this variation as well.



## Requirements

- Use the existing spam detector.
- Use an **IF Condition**.
- Use **OR Conditions**.
- Use an **Alarm**.



## Success Criteria

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONEY**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MonEy**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **money**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MoNeY**.

✅ Alarm turns ON when the message contains **MONey**.

✅ Alarm remains OFF for messages that do not contain these words.

steps.title: 6. 🧩 The Rule Problem
steps.body:
So far, we have been trying to detect the word:

**MONEY**


At first, this seemed easy.

We created one rule:

**IF Text = MONEY → Alarm**



But then spammers started using different variations:

- **Money**
- **money**
- **MonEy**
- **MoNeY**
- **MONey**
- **mONeY**
- **moneY**


For every new variation, we had to add another rule.

steps.title: 7. 🔄 More Variations, More Rules
steps.body:
## How Many Rules Do We Need?

The word **MONEY** has 5 letters.

Each letter can be **uppercase** or **lowercase**.

This means there are many possible variations.

Just to detect one word, we may need dozens of **IF Conditions**.

And we still might miss some variations.
steps.tip:
The word "MONEY" has 5 letters.

Each letter can be uppercase or lowercase.

This means there are:

2 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 2 = 32 possible variations.

Would you create 32 different IF Conditions just to detect one word?

steps.title: 8. 🚀 The Bigger Word Problem
steps.body:
## What About a Longer Word?

Now imagine we want to detect:

**CONGRATULATIONS**

Possible variations include:

- **Congratulations**
- **CONGRATULATIONS**
- **congratulations**
- **CoNgRaTuLaTiOnS**

And many more.

Would we create a new rule for every variation?

That would require a lot of IF Conditions.
steps.tip:
The word "CONGRATULATIONS" has 15 letters.

That means it can be written in:

"32,768" different ways.

Would you create 32,768 "IF Conditions" for a single word?

steps.title: 9. 💬 The Sentence Problem
steps.body:
## What About Full Sentences?

Now imagine we want to detect this spam message:

**Congratulations! You have won a free prize.**

A spammer could write:

- **Congrats! You won a reward.**
- **Claim your free gift now!**
- **You have been selected for a special offer!**

All of these messages have a similar meaning, but they use different words.

Would we need hundreds or even thousands of rules to detect every possible sentence?

steps.title: 10. 🐱 A Bigger Challenge
steps.body:
## Let's Try Something Different


Imagine we want a computer to detect a picture of a **CAT**.


We could create rules like:

- IF the animal has 2 ears
- IF the animal has 4 legs
- IF the animal has a tail
- IF the animal has whiskers


Then it must be a CAT.


But there is a problem.

A **DOG** can also have:

- 2 ears
- 4 legs
- A tail

Now we need even more rules.

How many rules would we need to correctly identify every CAT and every DOG?


🌟 In the next chapter, we will explore a different way to teach AI.

steps.title: 📌 Summary
steps.body:
## What We Learned

- Rules can help computers make decisions.
- A spam detector can be created using IF Conditions.
- Every new variation requires additional rules.
- Longer words need even more rules.
- Full sentences can be written in many different ways.
- As rules increase, systems become larger and harder to manage.


📌 Sometimes writing more rules is not the best solution.



🌟 What if we could teach AI without writing thousands of rules?

How would that work?

Let's find out in the next chapter.

steps.title: 🤔 When Rules Are Not Enough
steps.body:
In the last chapter, we discovered a problem.

Our spam detector could recognise:

**MONEY**

But when we changed it to:

**MonEy**

The rule stopped working.

To fix it, we had to create another rule.

Then another.

And another.


🤔 Imagine doing this for thousands of words.

Or millions of messages.

It would be almost impossible.

steps.title: A Big Question
steps.body:
If rules keep breaking...

Is there another way for machines to learn?

Can a machine learn from **examples** instead of being given every rule?

steps.title: The Beginning of Machine Learning
steps.body:
This is where **Machine Learning** begins.

Instead of writing rules ourselves, we give the machine examples.

The machine studies those examples and learns patterns.



## Example



Instead of writing:

**IF message contains MONEY → Spam**

We show the machine examples:

| Message | Answer |
|----------|----------|
| Win free money now | Spam |
| Claim your prize | Spam |
| Meeting at 3 PM | Not Spam |
| Bring your notebook tomorrow | Not Spam |



After seeing many examples:

🤔 The machine starts learning patterns on its own.

steps.title: Key Idea
steps.body:
✅ **Rule-Based AI learns from rules.**

✅ **Machine Learning learns from examples.**

✅ **Instead of telling the machine every rule, we show it examples and let it discover patterns.**

steps.title: Guided Lab 1: Collect Your First AI Dataset
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to collect and upload data for an AI system.



## Problem

An AI cannot learn to recognise **cats** and **dogs** unless it has examples to learn from.

Your job is to collect the data.



## Instructions



**Step 1:**
Search online for **10 images of cats**.

**Step 2:**
Download all **10 cat images** to your computer.

**Step 3:**
Search online for **10 images of dogs**.

**Step 4:**
Download all **10 dog images** to your computer.

**Step 5:**
Open **ABITA**.

**Step 6:**
Go to the **Data Panel**.

**Step 7:**
Click **Add Data**.

**Step 8:**
Upload all **20 images** into the **Data Bank**.

**Step 9:**
Check that all uploaded images appear in the **Data Bank**.




## Success Criteria


- ✅ 10 cat images downloaded
- ✅ 10 dog images downloaded
- ✅ All 20 images uploaded successfully
- ✅ All images appear in the Data Bank



## Expected Result


🤔 You have collected and uploaded data.

🤔 The AI still does not know which images are cats and which are dogs.

🤔 In the next lab, you will organise and label the data so it can be used for learning.

steps.title: Guided Lab 2: Create a Labelled Dataset
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to create labelled data for AI training.



## Problem

You have collected cat and dog images, but the AI does not know what each image contains.

Your job is to label every image correctly.



## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Open **ABITA**.

**Step 2:**
From the **Data Menu**, drag a **Label Data** block onto the canvas.

**Step 3:**
Double-click the **Label Data** block to open its **Properties Panel**.

**Step 4:**
In the **Labels** section, create two labels:

- Cat
- Dog

**Step 5:**
Select all images from the **Data Bank**.

**Step 6:**
Drag the selected images into the **Items** section of the **Label Data** block.

**Step 7:**
Assign the correct label to each image:

- Cat images → **Cat**
- Dog images → **Dog**

**Step 8:**
Use **Expand View** if you need a larger workspace for labelling.

**Step 9:**
In the **Save Dataset** section, enter the name:

**Training Data Cat & Dog**

**Step 10:**
Click **Save Data**.

**Step 11:**
Open the **Datasets** tab from the left panel.

**Step 12:**
Check that **Training Data Cat & Dog** appears in the list of saved datasets.



## Success Criteria

- ✅ A **Cat** label has been created
- ✅ A **Dog** label has been created
- ✅ All images have been labelled correctly
- ✅ The dataset is saved as **Training Data Cat & Dog**
- ✅ **Training Data Cat & Dog** appears in the **Datasets** tab



## Expected Result

🤔 You have created a labelled dataset.

🤔 Each image now has a correct answer attached to it.

🤔 The dataset is stored in the **Datasets** tab and is ready to be used for AI training.

steps.title: Guided Lab 3: Create Another Labelled Dataset
steps.instruction:
## Goal


- Practise creating labelled datasets for AI training.
- Learn why correct labels are important for teaching an AI model.



## Problem


Before an AI can learn, humans must organise and label the data.

Your job is to create a new labelled dataset using cat images.

This time, you will organise the cats into two groups:

- **Serious Cat**
- **Smiling Cat**



## Instructions


**Step 1:**
Search online for **10 images of serious cats**.

**Step 2:**
Download all **10 serious cat images** to your computer.

**Step 3:**
Search online for **10 images of smiling cats**.

**Step 4:**
Download all **10 smiling cat images** to your computer.

**Step 5:**
Open **ABITA**.

**Step 6:**
Go to the **Data Panel**.

**Step 7:**
Click **Add Data**.

**Step 8:**
Upload all **20 cat images** into the **Data Bank**.

**Step 9:**
From the **Data Menu**, drag a **Label Data** block onto the canvas.

**Step 10:**
Double-click the **Label Data** block to open its **Properties Panel**.

**Step 11:**
In the **Labels** section, create two labels:

- Serious Cat
- Smiling Cat

**Step 12:**
Select all uploaded images from the **Data Bank**.

**Step 13:**
Drag the selected images into the **Items** section of the **Label Data** block.

**Step 14:**
Assign the correct label to each image:

- Serious cat images → **Serious Cat**
- Smiling cat images → **Smiling Cat**

**Step 15:**
Use **Expand View** if you need a larger workspace for labelling.

**Step 16:**
In the **Save Dataset** section, enter the name:

**Training Data Serious Cats & Smiling Cats**

**Step 17:**
Click **Save Data**.

**Step 18:**
Open the **Datasets** tab from the left panel.

**Step 19:**
Check that **Training Data Serious Cats & Smiling Cats** appears in the list of saved datasets.



## Success Criteria


- ✅ A **Serious Cat** label has been created
- ✅ A **Smiling Cat** label has been created
- ✅ All images have been labelled correctly
- ✅ The dataset is saved as **Training Data Serious Cats & Smiling Cats**
- ✅ The dataset appears in the **Datasets** tab



## Expected Result


🤔 You have practised creating another labelled dataset.

🤔 Every image has been assigned a label by a human.

🤔 AI models learn from the labels we provide.

🤔 Creating good labelled datasets is an important step in building AI systems.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 1: Build a Lion and Tiger Dataset
steps.prompt:
## Problem

You are working as a **Data Labeler** for a wildlife technology company.

Your team is building a smart camera that can automatically recognise whether an animal is a **lion** or a **tiger**.

Before the AI can learn, it needs labelled data.

Your job is to collect and label the images that will be used to train the AI.



## Requirements

- Collect **10 images of lions**
- Collect **10 images of tigers**
- Upload all images into **ABITA**
- Create a **Label Data** block
- Create two labels:
  - Lion
  - Tiger
- Label all images correctly
- Save the dataset with the name:

**WildLifeTigerLion**



## Success Criteria

- ✅ A **Tiger** label has been created
- ✅ All images have been labelled correctly
- ✅ The dataset is saved as **WildLifeTigerLion**
- ✅ **WildLifeTigerLion** appears in the **Datasets** tab



## Expected Result

🤔 You have created a labelled dataset for a wildlife AI system.

🤔 Every image has been assigned the correct label.

🤔 The dataset is ready to be used for AI training.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 2: Build a Cars and Bikes Dataset
steps.prompt:

Your team is building an AI system that can automatically recognise **cars** and **bikes** from traffic camera images.

- Upload all images into **ABITA**
- Create a **Label Data** block
- Create two labels:
  - Car
  - Bike

**TrafficCarBike**

steps.title: Guided Lab 4: Create a Labelled Text Dataset
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to create a labelled dataset using text data.



## Problem

You are working as a **Data Labeler** for an email company.

Your team is building an AI system that can automatically recognise **Spam** and **Not Spam** messages.

Before the AI can be trained, it needs labelled text data.

Your job is to create the dataset.



## Labelled Dataset

Dataset Name : **Training Data SpamDetection**

| Text Message | Label |
|-------------|--------|
| Win free money now | Spam |
| Your meeting is at 3 PM | Not Spam |
| Claim your prize today | Spam |
| Please submit your homework | Not Spam |
| Get cheap loan instantly | Spam |
| Class will start at 10 AM | Not Spam |
| Congratulations, you won a gift | Spam |
| I will call you later | Not Spam |
| Click here to get cash | Spam |
| Bring your notebook tomorrow | Not Spam |



## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Open **ABITA**.

**Step 2:**
From the **Data Menu**, drag a **Label Data** block onto the canvas.

**Step 3:**
Double-click the **Label Data** block to open its properties.

**Step 4:**
Copy the labelled dataset provided above.

**Step 5:**
Click the **Paste Data** button.

**Step 6:**
Paste the dataset into the text area.

**Step 7:**
Create two labels:

- Spam
- Not Spam

**Step 8:**
Verify that all messages have been assigned the correct labels.

**Step 9:**
In the **Save Dataset** section, enter the name:

**Training Data SpamDetection**

**Step 10:**
Click **Save Data**.

**Step 11:**
Open the **Datasets** tab.

**Step 12:**
Check that **Training Data SpamDetection** appears in the list of saved datasets.



## Success Criteria

- ✅ A **Spam** label has been created
- ✅ A **Not Spam** label has been created
- ✅ All text messages have been labelled correctly
- ✅ The dataset is saved as **Training Data SpamDetection**
- ✅ **Training Data SpamDetection** appears in the **Datasets** tab



## Expected Result

🤔 You have created a labelled text dataset.

🤔 Data can be images or text.

🤔 AI systems can learn from text in the same way they learn from images.

🤔 The dataset is ready to be used for AI training.

steps.title: Challenge Lab 3: Create a News Classification Dataset
steps.prompt:
## Problem

You are working as a **Data Labeler** for a news company.

Your team is building an AI system that can automatically recognise **Sports News** and **Technology News** articles.

Before the AI can be trained, it needs labelled text data.

Your job is to create the dataset.



## Requirements

- Create a **Label Data** block.
- Create two labels:
  - Sports
  - Technology
- Copy the labelled dataset provided below.
- Paste the dataset into the **Label Data** block.
- Verify that all headlines have the correct labels.
- Save the dataset with the name:

**NewsClassificationData**



## Labelled Dataset

# Dataset Name: **NewsClassificationData**

| News Headline | Label |
|--------------|--------|
| Pakistan Wins T20 Series After Thrilling Final Match | Sports |
| Star Football Club Secures Victory in League Opener | Sports |
| National Cricket Team Announces New Captain | Sports |
| Tennis Champion Advances to Grand Slam Final | Sports |
| Local Marathon Attracts Record Number of Participants | Sports |
| Basketball Team Extends Winning Streak to Ten Games | Sports |
| Hockey Federation Reveals Schedule for Upcoming Tournament | Sports |
| Young Athlete Breaks National Sprint Record | Sports |
| International Football Tournament Kicks Off This Weekend | Sports |
| Cricket Board Launches New Youth Development Program | Sports |
| AI-Powered Assistant Introduces Advanced Language Features | Technology |
| Tech Company Unveils Next-Generation Smartphone | Technology |
| Researchers Develop More Efficient Solar Panel Technology | Technology |

## Success Criteria

- ✅ A **Technology** label has been created
- ✅ All headlines have been imported correctly
- ✅ All labels have been verified

🤔 The dataset is ready to be used for AI training.

steps.title: 🤔 The Problem
steps.body:
## The Problem

Imagine you want to teach an AI to recognise **cats** and **dogs**.

You have collected **20 images**:

- 10 Cat Images
- 10 Dog Images


Should we use all 20 images for training?

🤔 Not quite.

If the AI learns from all the images, how will we know whether it has really learned?

To solve this problem, we divide our data into two groups.

- 1. Training Data
- 2. Test Data

steps.title: 🎓 Training Data
steps.body:
# Training Data


**Training Data** is used to teach the AI.

The model looks at these examples and learns patterns.


## Example


- 8 Cat Images
- 8 Dog Images

**Total = 16 Images**

These images are used for learning.

✅ **Training Data = Data used to teach the AI.**

steps.title: 🧪 Test Data
steps.body:
# Test Data

**Test Data** is used after training.

The model has never seen these images before.

# Example

- 2 Cat Images
- 2 Dog Images

**Total = 4 Images**

These images are used to check whether the AI has learned successfully.

✅ **Test Data = Data used to check the AI.**



## Why Do We Need Test Data?

Imagine a student memorises practice questions before an exam.

They might do well on those exact questions, but that does not mean they truly understand the subject or can answer new ones.

In the same way, an AI should be tested using new examples it has never seen before.



✅ **Test Data helps us measure how well the AI performs on new data.**

steps.title: 📝 Quick Summary
steps.body:
## Quick Summary


| Dataset  Role (How will we use the data?) | Purpose |
|------------|----------|
| Training Data | Teach the AI |
| Test Data | Check if the AI learned |



👉 **Training Data helps the AI learn.**

👉 **Test Data helps us measure how well the AI performs.**

steps.title: Guided Lab 5: Create Training Data and Test Data
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to divide data into **Training Data** and **Test Data**.



## Problem

You are working as a **Data Labeler** for a furniture company.

Your team is building an AI system that can automatically recognise **Office Tables** and **Office Chairs** from product images.

Before the AI can be trained, the data must be divided into **Training Data** and **Test Data**.

Your job is to create both datasets.



## Instructions


# Collect the Data

**Step 1:**
Search online for **10 images of Office Tables**.

**Step 2:**
Download all **10 Office Table images** to your computer.

**Step 3:**
Search online for **10 images of Office Chairs**.

**Step 4:**
Download all **10 Office Chair images** to your computer.

**Step 5:**
Open **ABITA**.

**Step 6:**
Upload all **20 images** into the **Data Bank**.



# Create the Training Dataset

**Step 7:**
Drag a **Label Data** block onto the canvas.

**Step 8:**
Double-click the **Label Data** block to open its properties.

**Step 9:**
Create two labels:

- Office Table
- Office Chair

**Step 10:**
Select **8 Office Table images** and **8 Office Chair images** from the Data Bank.

**Step 11:**
Drag the selected images into the **Items** section.

**Step 12:**
Assign the correct label to each image.

**Step 13:**
Save the dataset with the name:

**Training Data OfficeFurniture**



# Create the Test Dataset

**Step 14:**
Drag another **Label Data** block onto the canvas.

**Step 15:**
Double-click the **Label Data** block to open its properties.

**Step 16:**
Create two labels:

- Office Table
- Office Chair

**Step 17:**
Select the remaining **2 Office Table images** and **2 Office Chair images** from the Data Bank.

**Step 18:**
Drag the selected images into the **Items** section.

**Step 19:**
Assign the correct label to each image.

**Step 20:**
Save the dataset with the name:

**Test Data OfficeFurniture**




# Verify Your Work

**Step 21:**
Open the **Datasets** tab.

**Step 22:**
Confirm that both datasets appear:

- Training Data OfficeFurniture
- Test Data OfficeFurniture



## Success Criteria


- ✅ A **Training Data OfficeFurniture** dataset has been created
- ✅ A **Test Data OfficeFurniture** dataset has been created
- ✅ Training Data contains **8 Office Tables** and **8 Office Chairs**
- ✅ Test Data contains **2 Office Tables** and **2 Office Chairs**
- ✅ Both datasets appear in the **Datasets** tab



## Expected Result

🤔 You have divided your data into **Training Data** and **Test Data**.

🤔 The **Training Data** will be used to teach the AI.

🤔 The **Test Data** will be used later to check how well the AI has learned.

🤔 Not all collected data should be used for training.

steps.title: Guided Lab 6: Train Your First AI Model
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to train an AI model using Training Data.

---


## Problem

You have already created a dataset called:

**Training Data OfficeFurniture**

This dataset contains labelled images of:

* Office Tables
* Office Chairs

Now it is time to teach an AI model using this data.

In ABITA, we will use an **Image Supervised Model**.

Your job is to train the model.

---


## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Open **ABITA**.


**Step 2:**
From the **Models** menu, drag an **Image Supervised Model** onto the canvas.


**Step 3:**
Open the **Datasets** tab. If the left panel is hidden, use the floating icon to open it.


**Step 4:**
Locate:

**Training Data OfficeFurniture**


**Step 5:**
Drag **Training Data OfficeFurniture** onto the canvas.


**Step 6:**
Double-click the **Image Supervised Model** to open its properties.

You should see:

**1 · Link Training Data**


**Step 7:**
Drag from the **violet dot** on **Training Data OfficeFurniture** to the **teal dot** on the **Image Supervised Model**.


**Step 8:**
Double click the model & verify that the model shows:

✅ Connected

**Training Data OfficeFurniture**

---


## Train the Model

**Step 9:**
If the model properties are not visible, double-click the **Image Supervised Model** again.

Under:

**2 · Train**

Click:

🚀 **Train Model**


**Step 10:**
Observe the training progress.

You may see messages such as:

* Initialising classifier...
* Loading AI...
* Training in progress...


**Step 11:**
Wait for the training process to complete.


**Step 12:**
Verify that the model displays:

✅ Training Complete

and shows the number of labels and images trained.

---


## Save the Model

**Step 13:**
Scroll to:

**3 · Save to My Models**


**Step 14:**
Enter the model name:

**OfficeFurnitureModel**


**Step 15:**
Click:

💾 **Save Name**


**Step 16:**
Open the **Models** tab on the left side menu.


**Step 17:**
Confirm that:

**OfficeFurnitureModel**

appears in the list of saved models.

---


## Success Criteria

✅ Training Data OfficeFurniture is added to the canvas

✅ Training Data OfficeFurniture is connected to the Image Supervised Model

✅ The model shows **Training Complete**

✅ OfficeFurnitureModel is saved

✅ OfficeFurnitureModel appears in the My Models tab

---


## Expected Result

🤔 The model has learned from the examples in the Training Data.

🤔 The model has learned patterns for recognising Office Tables and Office Chairs.

🤔 The model is now ready to make predictions.

🤔 Training teaches the AI, but it does not tell us how well the AI has learned.

👉 In the next lab, you will test the model using **Test Data OfficeFurniture**.

steps.title: 📖 How Do We Know If Our AI Learned?
steps.body:
In the last lab, we trained our model.

The model studied examples of:

- Office Tables
- Office Chairs

But one important question remains.

🤔 Did the model actually learn?

To find out, we must test it using data it has never seen before.

This is called: **Test Data**

steps.title: 📊 Understanding Test Results
steps.body:
When a model is tested, it makes predictions.

Some predictions may be:

✅ **Correct**

Some predictions may be:

❌ **Incorrect**



## 📈 Accuracy

Accuracy tells us how many predictions were correct.

| Images Tested | Correct Predictions | Accuracy |
|--------------|--------------------|----------|
| 4 | 4 | 100% |
| 4 | 3 | 75% |
| 4 | 2 | 50% |

The higher the accuracy, the better the model performed.

steps.title: 🤔 Can Models Make Mistakes?
steps.body:
# 🤔 Can Models Make Mistakes?

**Yes.**

Even trained models can sometimes make mistakes.

For now, it is enough to know that not every prediction will always be correct.


👉 In future chapters, we will explore why models make mistakes and how they can be improved.



# 🚀 Looking Ahead

Now it is time to test our model and see how well it performs.

steps.title: Guided Lab 7: Test Your First AI Model
steps.instruction:
## Goal

Learn how to test an AI model using **Test Data**.




## Problem

You have successfully trained:

**OfficeFurnitureModel**

using:

**Training Data OfficeFurniture**

But how do we know if the model has learned well?

To find out, we must test the model using data it has never seen before.

Your job is to test the model using:

**Test Data OfficeFurniture**




## Instructions

**Step 1:**
Open **ABITA**.


**Step 2:**
Open the **Models** tab.


**Step 3:**
Locate:

**OfficeFurnitureModel**


**Step 4:**
Drag **OfficeFurnitureModel** onto the canvas.

**Step 5:**
Open the **Datasets** tab.


**Step 6:**
Locate:

**Test Data OfficeFurniture**


**Step 7:**
Drag **Test Data OfficeFurniture** onto the canvas.


**Step 8:**
Double-click **OfficeFurnitureModel** to open its properties.

You should see:

**4 · Test Model**


**Step 9:**
Drag from the **amber dot** on **Test Data OfficeFurniture** to the **amber dot** on **OfficeFurnitureModel**.


**Step 10:**
Double-click the model again.


**Step 11:**
Verify that the model shows:

🗂️ **Test Data OfficeFurniture**

and displays the number of test images.





## Run the Test

**Step 12:**
Click:

🔬 **Run Test**


**Step 13:**
Wait for the testing process to complete.

You may see:

**Running inference...**


**Step 14:**
Verify that the model displays results similar to:

- ✅ Images Tested
- ✅ Accuracy Percentage
- ✅ Correct Predictions




## View Detailed Results

**Step 15:**
Click:

↗ **Expand Results**


**Step 16:**
Review the results for each image.

Observe:

- Predicted Label
- Actual Label
- Confidence Score
- Correct Predictions
- Incorrect Predictions





## Success Criteria


- ✅ OfficeFurnitureModel is added to the canvas
- ✅ Test Data OfficeFurniture is connected to the model
- ✅ Testing completes successfully
- ✅ An accuracy score is displayed
- ✅ Detailed results can be viewed





## Expected Result

🤔 The model has been tested using data it has never seen before.

🤔 Test Data helps us measure how well the AI has learned.

🤔 Accuracy tells us how many predictions were correct.

🤔 A trained model is not always a good model.

🤔 Testing helps us understand the strengths and weaknesses of a model.

steps.title: 📖 Understanding Your Results
steps.body:
You have now tested your AI model using data it had never seen before.

Take a moment to look at your results.



## 🤔 Were Your Results Different?



Some students may achieve:

✅ **High Accuracy**


While others may achieve:

⚠️ **Lower Accuracy**

This is completely normal.

steps.title: 🔍 An Important Observation
steps.instruction:
# Not all AI models perform the same way.

Even when two students follow similar steps, their models may produce different results.

A trained model is not always a perfect model.

That is why testing is important.

Without testing, we would never know how well a model has learned.





## ❓ A Big Question

If every student followed similar steps...

🤔 Why did some models perform better than others?

🤔 Why do some models make mistakes?

🤔 Can a model be improved?

These are important questions.


👉 In future chapters, we will explore how models learn, why they make mistakes, and how they can be improved.

steps.title: 📖 What Did We Just Build?
steps.body:
You have now completed your first **Machine Learning Project**.

Along the way, you worked like a real AI team.


You:


- ✅ Collected data
- ✅ Labelled data
- ✅ Created Training Data
- ✅ Created Test Data
- ✅ Trained an AI model
- ✅ Tested an AI model


## 🔄 The Machine Learning Process



**1️⃣ Collect Data**

⬇

**2️⃣ Label Data**

⬇

**3️⃣ Create Training Data**

⬇

**4️⃣ Create Test Data**

⬇

**5️⃣ Train the Model**

⬇

**6️⃣ Test the Model**

steps.title: 🏆 Challenge Lab 4: Wildlife Animal Classifier
steps.prompt:
## Problem

You are working as a **Machine Learning Engineer** for a wildlife research company.

Your team wants to build an AI system that can automatically recognise:

- Lions
- Tigers

Your job is to create the datasets, train the model, and test the model.




## Requirements

- Collect **10 Lion images**
- Collect **10 Tiger images**
- Create:
  - **Training Data WildLife**
  - **Test Data WildLife**
- Train an **Image Supervised Model**
- Save the model as:

**WildLifeModel**

- Test the model using **Test Data WildLife**





## Success Criteria

- ✅ Training and Test datasets created
- ✅ Model trained successfully
- ✅ Model tested successfully
- ✅ Accuracy results displayed





## Expected Result

🤔 You have built a complete AI system from start to finish.

🤔 The AI has learned to recognise lions and tigers using examples.

🤔 The model has been tested using data it has never seen before.

🤔 The accuracy score shows how well the model performs on new data.

steps.title: 🏆 Challenge Lab 5: Traffic Vehicle Classifier
steps.prompt:
## Problem

You are working as a **Machine Learning Engineer** for a smart traffic company.

Your team wants to build an AI system that can automatically recognise:

- Cars
- Bikes


Your job is to create the datasets, train the model, and test the model.





## Requirements

- Collect **10 Car images**
- Collect **10 Bike images**
- Create:
  - **Training Data Traffic**
  - **Test Data Traffic**
- Train an **Image Supervised Model**
- Save the model as:

**TrafficModel**

- Test the model using **Test Data Traffic**





## Success Criteria

- ✅ Training and Test datasets created
- ✅ Model trained successfully
- ✅ Model tested successfully
- ✅ Accuracy results displayed





## Expected Result

🤔 You have built a complete AI system from start to finish.

🤔 The AI has learned to recognise cars and bikes using examples.

🤔 The model has been tested using data it has never seen before.

🤔 The accuracy score shows how well the model performs on new data.
